mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances when the trigger activates, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows zero marginal predictive value in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and short-term equity returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises more than 0.4% month-over-month, inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding the threshold, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when payrolls rise but unemployment also rises, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon following each data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day equity returns, or the signal reverses direction during stagflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month consecutive decline in the index of industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production declines due to temporary seasonal adjustments, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.
